title: 3.4.6 fix: 'default' instance name collides with OMZ shell function
description: The default value of --name in install.sh is 'default'. Oh My Zsh defines a no-op 'default()' shell function in ~/.oh-my-zsh/lib/functions.zsh that takes precedence over PATH lookup, silently shadowing the ~/.local/bin/default wrapper for every zsh+OMZ user. Three-part fix needed: (1) add 'default' (and other common OMZ function names: home, list, run, get, set, test, build, etc. — audit ~/.oh-my-zsh/lib/functions.zsh) to validate_instance_name blocklist in lib/validate.sh; (2) change the implicit default INSTANCE_NAME from 'default' to something non-colliding OR make --name mandatory; (3) runtime collision check in personal_cmd_install that uses 'command -v' + a zsh function check (whence -a in user's $SHELL) before generating the wrapper, warn or fail with copy-paste fix. Verified on Station11 2026-05-21 — see memory omz-default-collision. Workaround for affected users: invoke wrapper via full path '~/.local/bin/<name>' or 'command <name>'.

title: Phase 3.4.6 — Personal command wrapper at ~/.local/bin/<name>
description: Generate ~/.local/bin/<name> wrapper + ensure PATH on install, remove on uninstall. Spec: .planning/phases/phase-3-tasks/3.4.6-personal-cmd.md

value: Phase 3.4.6 followup Claudify-e4a (OMZ default-name collision) ✅ shipped 2026-05-25 (commits 4f3b5d0 + cfbaf1a). Three-part fix: (1) lib/validate.sh blocklists 'default' as instance name, (2) lib/layout.sh defaults INSTANCE_NAME to $(id -un) with claudify-bot fallback, (3) lib/personal-cmd.sh adds personal_cmd_collision_check (PATH binary + zsh function detection, warn-don't-fail). Verified on Station11: 'david' (whoami) auto-picked, wrapper at ~/.local/bin/david works, david doctor 34/34 green in fresh zsh. Bonus: fixed phase-3-tasks/README markers for 3.4.5.1/.2. Bonus 2: discovered + fixed SIGPIPE bug — see sigpipe-claude-version memory.

Diagnostic: if ~/.claudify-<name>/.install-partial exists AFTER install.sh supposedly finished, the install was interrupted (Ctrl-C, network drop, autoinstall validation crash, etc.) and didn't reach final_summary. Tell-tale signs of interrupted install: (1) .install-partial present, (2) wrapper at ~/.local/bin/<name> missing, (3) no '# Claudify PATH —' marker in ~/.bashrc, (4) registry entry exists but per-instance ~/.claudify-<name>/claudify.json missing. Recovery: 'bash <(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/didi6135/Claudify/main/uninstall.sh) --name <name> --yes' then reinstall. DO NOT trust a stale ~/test/uninstall.sh — use main's current code.

Phase 3 status as of 2026-05-12: 8 of 13 sub-tasks DONE. Shipped (with dates): 3.1 uninstall (04-23), 3.2 update.sh + --preserve-state (04-24), 3.3 starter CLAUDE.md persona (04-24), 3.4.1 repo skeleton (04-28), 3.4.2 lib/steps.sh split (04-30), 3.4.2.1 resume install (05-04), 3.4.3 engine abstraction (05-04), 3.4.4 manifest files (05-04), 3.4.5 multi-instance flat layout + Tier-1 hardening (05-11), 3.4.5.1 skill data substrate + lib/memory.sh (05-12), 3.4.5.2 engine contract 8→10 fns (05-12). Remaining in Phase 3: 3.4.6 personal cmd (~45m), 3.4.7 migration (~30m), 3.4.8 docs sync (~30m), 3.4.9 containerize (~7hr), 3.5 backup/restore TS (~3-4hr). Then Phase 4 security.

Latent bug fixed 2026-05-25 in cfbaf1a: '$(cmd --version 2>/dev/null | head -1)' under 'set -o pipefail' is a SIGPIPE race. head closes the pipe after one line; cmd is still flushing → SIGPIPE → 141 → pipefail → set -e halts. Symptom on Station11: install log ends at '✓ service is running', registry has entry but per-instance claudify.json missing, .install-partial left behind, wrapper at ~/.local/bin/<name> never created. Lesson: any 'cmd | head -N' or 'cmd | awk NR==N{exit}' pattern under pipefail is fragile. Pattern fix: capture full output (no pipe), trim via parameter expansion ('var="$(cmd)" || var=""; var="${var%%$'\n'*}"'). Audit checklist: grep -rn '| head' lib/ before any install-path change.

Station11 test server: ssh -i ~/.ssh/station11 -p 43532 david@77.42.21.117. Use for all round-trip tests. Working dir on server: /home/david. To run user systemctl commands over ssh, export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/$(id -u) first.

Per ADR 0006 (2026-05-11): per-instance layout is FLAT (~/.claudify-<name>/) NOT nested (~/.claudify/instances/<n>/). Side-car registry at ~/.claudify-registry.json. Each instance is fully self-contained at its top-level dir. Older docs/specs (especially Phase 4-7) may still reference the nested layout — update the prose, not the code (code uses $CLAUDIFY_INSTANCE_DIR which resolves correctly under either form). Service name: claudify-<name>.service. CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR points at <instance>/claude/ for per-instance Claude state.

Mount-namespace isolation (ProtectHome=tmpfs + BindPaths) is PERMANENTLY ABANDONED. Station11 testing on 2026-05-10 proved AppArmor on Ubuntu 24.04 blocks unprivileged userns mount operations — directives parse, systemctl show reports them, but /proc/<pid>/ns/mnt shows no namespace was created. 3.6.2 spec marked SUPERSEDED; original 45-min task reduced to 5-min cleanup (strip the directives so the unit doesn't claim isolation it can't deliver). Real cross-instance isolation lives in 3.4.9 containerize (kernel-enforced namespaces). See ADR 0006 appendix.

BUG (open): Oh My Zsh defines a no-op shell function 'default()' in ~/.oh-my-zsh/lib/functions.zsh. zsh resolves functions BEFORE PATH binaries, so 'default doctor' silently invokes the OMZ function (returns 0 with no output) instead of ~/.local/bin/default. This breaks the 3.4.6 wrapper UX for every zsh+OMZ user when --name is omitted (default name = 'default'). Workaround: 'command default ...' or full path '~/.local/bin/default ...'. Open code fix tracked separately — see bd issue. Verified on Station11 2026-05-21 with whence -a: 'default is a shell function from /home/david/.oh-my-zsh/lib/functions.zsh' + '/home/david/.local/bin/default'.

Skill data substrate (Phase 3.4.5.1, shipped 2026-05-12) — every skill gets a private data dir at ~/.claudify-<name>/data/<skill-id>/ (mode 700, persists across update.sh + --reset-config). Operator-facing env var: ${CLAUDIFY_SKILL_DATA} matches Anthropic's ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} so cross-ecosystem skills work. lib/memory.sh exposes 5 helpers: memory_dir, memory_path, memory_assert_write, memory_assert_read, memory_export_env. Asserts are accident-prevention (typo guard for db-names), not a sandbox — single-user trust per ADR 0006. Manifest gained skills[].memory.{writes,reads} schema; writes/reads accept string OR array form. manifest_set_skill + manifest_get_skill_memory are the helpers.

On Station11 (post-2026-05-21): ~/test/autoinstall.sh defaults INSTANCE_NAME='david' (not 'default' — collides with OMZ shell function in ~/.oh-my-zsh/lib/functions.zsh). It ends with 'exec $SHELL -l' (only on TTY) so the user lands in a fresh login shell with the wrapper immediately callable — no manual rehash or source needed. Override via INSTANCE_NAME=foo / INSTALL_SRC=file:/abs/path env vars. Pre-3.4.6 backup at ~/test/autoinstall.sh.pre-3.4.6.bak (single-instance era — do NOT run). Sibling test scripts ~/test/{uninstall,doctor,update}.sh are thin curl-forwarders to https://raw.githubusercontent.com/didi6135/Claudify/main/{cmd}.sh; their pre-3.4.6 standalone copies are at *.pre-3.4.6.bak. $SHELL on david's account: /usr/bin/zsh.

Engine adapter contract is 10 functions (since 3.4.5.2 on 2026-05-12, was 8). Defined in lib/engines/<id>.sh — implement all 10, can be no-ops. The 10: engine_install, engine_seed_state <wsdir>, engine_install_channel_plugin <name>, engine_auth_check, engine_auth_setup, engine_run_args, engine_status, engine_uninstall, engine_memory_setup (no-op stub today; Phase 5.0b fills with 'claude mcp add claudify-memory ...'), engine_apply_persona <text> (marker-bracketed write into <instance>/workspace/CLAUDE.md, idempotent). Today's only adapter: claude-code.sh. New adapter (Gemini/Codex/etc.) = drop a new lib/engines/<id>.sh implementing all 10.

Four non-negotiable architectural invariants (from CLAUDE.md). Breaking requires ADR + explicit operator approval, not just a PR. 1) Engine-agnostic core: only lib/engines/<id>.sh references Claude-specific stuff (binary 'claude', ~/.claude/, Claude tool names). install.sh, lib/*.sh outside engines/, src/, tests/ must stay generic. 2) Clean uninstall: uninstall.sh leaves the system as if Claudify never ran. Everything under ~/.claudify-<name>/ or ~/.config/systemd/user/. No /etc, no /var, no env-var pollution, no orphan PATH entries. 3) Substrate independence: memory/persona/conversation data lives in plain files + SQLite at known paths. Operator can inspect/edit/backup with cat/sqlite3/vi even with every Claudify process dead. MCP/skills are glue, not gates. 4) Single-user trust model: no malicious-skill defenses, no per-skill sandboxing, no permission systems. Isolation is for accident prevention, not adversaries.

lib/engines/claude-code.sh is 344 lines (above the CLAUDE.md ≤300 budget) on PURPOSE. The 'no cross-adapter sourcing' architectural invariant means the 10-function engine contract has to live in one file per engine — splitting it would break the invariant. Header comment in the file + .planning/phases/phase-3-tasks/3.4.5.2-summary.md document the exception. Don't 'fix' it by splitting.

For non-interactive client install (round-trip tests, automation): pre-seed three files BEFORE running install with --preserve-state --non-interactive, so secrets never appear in process argv. Paths: ~/.claudify-<name>/credentials.env (chmod 600, line: CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=sk-ant-oat01-...), ~/.claudify-<name>/channels/telegram/.env (chmod 600, line: TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=...), ~/.claudify-<name>/channels/telegram/access.json (chmod 644, JSON with dmPolicy/allowFrom/groups/pending). Then bash install.sh --name <n> --preserve-state --non-interactive.

Phase 3.4.6 personal command wrapper at ~/.local/bin/<name>: ✅ shipped 2026-05-20 (code commit 87c807a, docs 1a6768a). 4 helpers in lib/personal-cmd.sh (159 lines): personal_cmd_install/uninstall/ensure_path/clean_path. install.sh calls personal_cmd_install after manifest writes; uninstall.sh removes the wrapper + cleans rc-file PATH marker (both .bashrc and .zshrc) when registry empties. Wrapper subcommands: doctor/update/uninstall (bash <(curl …) from main) + status/logs/start/stop/restart (local systemctl --user claudify-<name>). Station11 round-trip: 34/34 doctor checks green.

Phase numbering reorg on 2026-05-12. Pipeline now: Phase 3 (lifecycle) → Phase 4 (security, was 3.6.x) → Phase 5 (memory MCP, was 4.0a/0b/1/1.1/4) → Phase 6 (skills, was 4.2/3/5/6) → Phase 7 (observability, was Phase 5). Renumber map: 3.6.X→4.X, 4.0a→5.0a, 4.0b→5.0b, 4.1→5.1, 4.1.1→5.1.1, 4.4→5.2, 4.2→6.1, 4.3→6.2, 4.5→6.3, 4.6→6.4. Old numbers still appear in commits before 5ec3dde. Each new phase has its own folder under .planning/phases/ with README.md status board.
